acceptableAlternatives: Academy Award for Best Animated Feature
quizQuestion: Spirited Away (2001) became the first anime to win an Academy Award — for which category?
explanation: Spirited Away won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature at the 75th Oscars in 2003, the first and for many years the only anime to win in that category. It was directed by Hayao Miyazaki and produced by Studio Ghibli.

quizQuestion: Which anime film finally surpassed Spirited Away as Japan's highest-grossing film in 2020?
explanation: Spirited Away held the record as Japan's highest-grossing film from 2001 until 2020, when Demon Slayer: Mugen Train (ufotable) earned approximately ¥40.4 billion domestically. Spirited Away itself had previously displaced Titanic from that record.

quizQuestion: In what year was Studio Ghibli co-founded by Hayao Miyazaki, Isao Takahata, and Toshio Suzuki?
explanation: Studio Ghibli was founded in 1985 in Tokyo, Japan, following the success of Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind. The studio's name refers to the Italian word for a hot Saharan wind, reflecting Miyazaki's love of aviation.

distractors: Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind
quizQuestion: What was the first official film produced by Studio Ghibli, released in 1986?
explanation: Castle in the Sky (Laputa: Castle in the Sky) was Studio Ghibli's first official production, directed by Hayao Miyazaki and released in 1986. Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind (1984) predates the studio but was instrumental in its founding.

quizQuestion: Totoro, the lovable forest spirit from the 1988 film, became the official mascot and logo of which studio?
explanation: My Neighbor Totoro (1988), directed by Hayao Miyazaki, introduced the iconic Totoro character who became Studio Ghibli's mascot and appears in the studio's logo. The film was originally released as a double feature with Grave of the Fireflies.

distractors: Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind
quizQuestion: Which 1997 Miyazaki film briefly held the record as Japan's highest-grossing film before being surpassed by Titanic?
explanation: Princess Mononoke (1997) earned ¥19.3 billion at the Japanese box office, temporarily becoming Japan's highest-grossing film ever — until Titanic later that year. The film was a landmark for mature animated storytelling and environmental themes.

quizQuestion: Who directed both My Neighbor Totoro (1988) and Spirited Away (2001)?
explanation: Hayao Miyazaki is the co-founder of Studio Ghibli and director of its most beloved films including My Neighbor Totoro, Spirited Away, Princess Mononoke, and Howl's Moving Castle. He is widely regarded as one of the greatest animation directors of all time.

quizQuestion: Who directed Grave of the Fireflies (1988), the anti-war Ghibli film often shown in Japanese schools?
explanation: Grave of the Fireflies was directed by Isao Takahata, co-founder of Studio Ghibli. The film depicts two orphaned siblings struggling to survive in Japan during World War II and is widely regarded as one of the most powerful anti-war films ever made.

correctAnswer: The Tale of the Princess Kaguya
quizQuestion: Which 2013 Isao Takahata film is known for its distinctive hand-drawn watercolor animation style and received an Oscar nomination?
explanation: The Tale of the Princess Kaguya (2013) was Isao Takahata's final film and received an Academy Award nomination for Best Animated Feature. It retells the ancient Japanese folk tale Taketori Monogatari using a distinctive sketchy, watercolor animation style.

distractors: The Girl Who Leapt Through Time
quizQuestion: Which 2016 Makoto Shinkai film earned $380 million worldwide and became the highest-grossing anime film globally at the time of its release?
explanation: Your Name (Kimi no Na wa, 2016), directed by Makoto Shinkai, grossed approximately $380 million worldwide, making it the highest-grossing anime film in the world at that time. The body-swap romance became a cultural phenomenon in Japan and internationally.

quizQuestion: Your Name, Weathering with You, and Suzume were all produced by which animation studio?
explanation: CoMix Wave Films has produced all of Makoto Shinkai's major theatrical films, including Your Name (2016), Weathering with You (2019), and Suzume (2022). The studio specializes in high-quality animation with detailed backgrounds and atmospheric lighting.

quizQuestion: Which director of Your Name (2016) is often nicknamed 'the next Miyazaki'?
explanation: Makoto Shinkai gained the nickname 'the next Miyazaki' after the massive success of Your Name (2016), which broke box office records for anime worldwide. His distinctive visual style features hyper-detailed backgrounds and emotionally-charged atmospheric lighting.

quizQuestion: Which director's 1997 film Perfect Blue reportedly influenced Darren Aronofsky's Black Swan and Requiem for a Dream?
explanation: Satoshi Kon directed Perfect Blue (1997), a psychological thriller about a pop idol whose grip on reality unravels. Darren Aronofsky reportedly purchased the rights to use specific shots from the film in Requiem for a Dream (2000) and cited it as an influence on Black Swan (2010).

distractors: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
quizQuestion: Satoshi Kon's 2006 film Paprika is widely credited as an influence on which Christopher Nolan blockbuster about dream infiltration?
explanation: Paprika (2006), directed by Satoshi Kon, depicts a device that allows therapists to enter patients' dreams. Christopher Nolan has acknowledged its influence on Inception (2010), and many film critics have noted striking visual and thematic parallels between the two films.

quizQuestion: At what age did visionary director Satoshi Kon pass away from pancreatic cancer in 2010?
explanation: Satoshi Kon died on August 24, 2010, at age 46 from pancreatic cancer, leaving behind four feature films — Perfect Blue, Millennium Actress, Tokyo Godfathers, and Paprika — and the TV series Paranoia Agent. His passing cut short a profoundly innovative career.

quizQuestion: Which 1995 anime film directed by Mamoru Oshii is widely credited as a major influence on The Matrix?
explanation: Ghost in the Shell (1995), directed by Mamoru Oshii, is a cyberpunk film exploring consciousness and identity in a future where humans merge with machines. The Wachowskis cited it as a primary visual and thematic influence on The Matrix (1999).

quizQuestion: Who directed Akira (1988), the landmark cyberpunk anime film that was groundbreaking for its massive production budget?
explanation: Akira (1988) was directed and written by Katsuhiro Otomo, based on his own manga series. With a production budget of ¥1.1 billion — unprecedented for anime at the time — it featured sophisticated animation and helped bring anime to global audiences.

quizQuestion: In what year was Akira, the landmark cyberpunk anime directed by Katsuhiro Otomo, released?
explanation: Akira was released in Japan on July 16, 1988. The same year also saw the release of My Neighbor Totoro and Grave of the Fireflies, making 1988 an extraordinary year for Japanese animation. Akira is set in a dystopian Neo-Tokyo in 2019.

distractors: The Tale of the Princess Kaguya
Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind
quizQuestion: Which 2023 Miyazaki film won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature in 2024?
explanation: The Boy and the Heron (2023), directed by Hayao Miyazaki and produced by Studio Ghibli, won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature at the 2024 ceremony. It represented Miyazaki's return to feature filmmaking after what was announced as his retirement following The Wind Rises (2013).

quizQuestion: Which studio produced Demon Slayer: Mugen Train (2020), which became the highest-grossing Japanese film of all time?
explanation: Demon Slayer: Mugen Train (2020) was produced by ufotable and earned approximately ¥40.4 billion in Japan, surpassing Spirited Away as the highest-grossing Japanese film ever at the time. ufotable is known for its fluid action sequences blending 2D and 3D animation.

quizQuestion: Who founded Studio Chizu and directed The Girl Who Leapt Through Time (2006) and Wolf Children (2012)?
explanation: Mamoru Hosoda directed The Girl Who Leapt Through Time (2006) and Summer Wars (2009) while at Madhouse, then founded his own studio, Studio Chizu, where he made Wolf Children (2012), The Boy and the Beast (2015), Mirai (2018), and Belle (2021).

correctAnswer: Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind
quizQuestion: Which 1984 Miyazaki film's commercial success directly led to the founding of Studio Ghibli?
explanation: Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind (1984) was produced by Topcraft before Studio Ghibli existed, but its box office success gave Miyazaki and Takahata the leverage and funding to establish Studio Ghibli in 1985. The film is often considered an unofficial Ghibli film.

quizQuestion: What is the name of the ten-year-old protagonist who works in a spirit bathhouse in Spirited Away (2001)?
explanation: Chihiro Ogino is the ten-year-old protagonist of Spirited Away who, after her parents are transformed into pigs, takes a job at a spirit bathhouse run by the witch Yubaba. She is given the work name 'Sen' by Yubaba as a way to suppress her identity.

quizQuestion: In Princess Mononoke (1997), what is the name of the human girl raised by wolves who fights to protect the forest?
explanation: San, nicknamed 'Princess Mononoke' (meaning spirit or monster princess), was raised by the wolf goddess Moro after her human parents abandoned her. She serves as a fierce protector of the forest against industrialization, and forms a complex bond with the film's hero Ashitaka.

quizQuestion: In what year was Spirited Away released in Japan, where it became the country's highest-grossing film at the time?
explanation: Spirited Away was released in Japan on July 20, 2001, and earned ¥31.7 billion domestically, making it the highest-grossing Japanese film at the time. It received wide international release in 2002–2003 and won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature in 2003.
